decisions.title: Productize Canvas vs Keep as Power User Feature
decisions.context: Canvas documentation (DOC-33) is being written, but unclear if Canvas is ready for GA or remains beta. Affects resource allocation for Canvas vs Explore V2.

arguments.for: Canvas enables advanced multi-plot analysis, deepens embed with power users, aligns with 'Embed Deeper' pillar
arguments.against: Explore V2 is the primary vehicle for 'Nail the Core'. Canvas may fragment user experience and dilute focus.

decisions.title: Prioritize Feature Flags Infrastructure Investment
decisions.context: ENG-8723 (database-backed flags) and ENG-9122 (FE prototypes) suggest major feature flag work. This infrastructure enables faster experimentation but diverts from customer-facing features.

arguments.for: Better feature flags = faster iteration on Explore V2, Canvas, Python Rules. Enables 'Build the Moat' through faster learning cycles.
arguments.against: Infrastructure work delays direct customer value. Should we buy vs build?

decisions.title: Commit to Sift-Horizons Integration Timeline
decisions.context: ENG-10002 is in flight. Horizons integration could be key differentiator (Build the Moat) but scope/timeline unclear. Affects Q2/Q3 roadmap.
decisions.blockedBy: Need Horizons team commitment on API stability and integration scope

arguments.for: Unique integration creates switching costs (moat). Expands addressable use cases.
arguments.against: External dependency risk. May distract from core Explore V2 and Python Rules improvements.

decisions.title: Scale vs Stabilize: Load Testing Strategy
decisions.context: ENG-10037 targeting 5M pts/sec, but multiple bugs (ENG-10115, ENG-10117, ENG-10114) suggest stability issues. Do we push scale or fix foundations?

arguments.for: Scale targets support enterprise customers (Embed Deeper). Performance is a moat.
arguments.against: Bugs erode trust. Stability issues may prevent capturing decisions (North Star metric). Fix before scaling.

decisions.title: Documentation Investment Level for Q2
decisions.context: DOC-33, DOC-21, DOC-1 suggest major docs push. ENG-10080 for SiftStream 0.9.0. How much PM/Eng time for docs vs features?

arguments.for: Docs directly increase Decisions Captured (customers can't use what they don't understand). Reduces support burden.
arguments.against: Docs are resource-intensive. Could hire tech writer instead of using eng/PM time.

keyResults.name: Explore V2 adoption: 70% of active users
Core workflows (plot, filter, annotate) bug rate < 2%
Python Rules adoption: 40% of power users

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy meeting load (80% of work hours). Only 8 free hours for deep work on strategic decisions. Three 1:1s today suggest urgent people issues or critical unblocks needed.
alerts: Meeting load at 80% - risk of reactive vs strategic work
Only 2 focus blocks this week - insufficient for decision quality
5 pending decisions require research/analysis time not in calendar
